prose: The positive call rests on curated CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence [E1], the diagnostic anchor for this evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis. Mechanistically, the molecular initiating event is grounded in MitoCarta3.0 Complex-I Q-site subunits, with the adverse outcome expressed in the Kamath 2022 SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population [E2]—a cell-type–specific coherence linking energetic failure to selective neuronal loss. An attached AOP pathway lends structural plausibility to this initiation-to-outcome chain [E1]. Brain-penetrance modeling supports plausible CNS exposure, a necessary precondition rather than proof of harm [E5]. Human corroboration comes from classic case-series describing causal parkinsonism [E4], which strengthens but does not establish mechanistic causation. ToxCast coverage is thin, consistent with MPTP as a research reagent, so bioactivity is treated as anti-diagnostic and non-decisive here [E3]. Reflecting sole reliance on curated mechanism, confidence is held at the curated_anchored_only tier, appropriately hedged given the thin assay corroboration.

citations.detail: MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
